rulings.Case Title: YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL VS. CRAIG K MARTIN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 1: PLAINTIFF YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL'S Motion To Quash Service Denied. Cross-Defendant Yvonne Eashman-Sewell has generally appeared in this action by filing a demurrer. Therefore, it is too late to file a motion to quash. (See Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. Sec. 418.10(e)(3).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Interim Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 2: Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court's Interim Discovery Order The Court's own motion to reconsider its discovery order, filed November 27, 2013, is off calendar as moot. On December 6, 2013, Defendants' filed a Notice of Defendants' Withdrawal of Second Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Deposition Testimony and Accompanying Request for Production of Documents and Things, as to Demand for Production of Documents no. 17 Only and Request to take December 31, 2013 Hearing Off Calendar. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: DEMOCRAT PRINTING AND LITHOGRAPHY COMPANY, VS. JOSEPH CONNELLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On First Amedned Petition For Order Compelling Arb
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 4: PETITIONER DEMOCRAT PRINTING AND LITHOGRAPHY COMPANY'S First Amedned Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration Hearing required. Counsel are requested to be prepared to address: 1. the status of the bankruptcy proceeding filed by Joseph Connelly and 2. the effect of the bankruptcy filing on this pending matter and on the case of Connelly v. Holland (Case CGC-12-523731). =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO ADVISORS LLC, FORMERLY KNOWN AS VS. KIT PATRICK JORY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 5: PETITIONER WELLS FARGO ADVISORS LLC'S, formerly known as WACHOVIA SECURITIES LLC, Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award This motion is off calendar. Insufficient service per CCP § 1005 and CCP § 1290.2, which requires at least 16 court days for notice of hearing. The proof of service states this motion was mailed on December 18, 2013, only eight court days prior to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: WESTERN MANAGEMENT PARTNERS VS. GRADE A CABINET AND MATERIALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 6: DEFENDANT GRADE A CABINET AND MATERIALS' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD This motion is off calendar. Insufficient service per CCP § 1005, which requires at least 16 court days for notice of hearing, plus five additional days for service by mail. The proof of service states this motion was mailed on December 3, 2013, which only allowed for three additional days for mailing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: TERRY WILLIAM IRELAND et al VS. GORDON & REES LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton To Compel Written Responses To Discovery, Production Of Documents, To Establish Admissions And For Sanctions Against Pltfs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 1: CROSS COMPLAINANT ROPERS, MAJESKI, KOHN & BENTLEY PC'S Motion To Compel Written Responses To Discovery, Production Of Documents, To Establish Admissions And For Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is unopposed. Defendant's motion and request for sanctions in the sum of $1,500.00 is in all respects granted except the date by which plaintiff must respond to all discovery is on or before January 31, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton For Protective Orderstaying Or, Alternatively, Limiting Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 8: DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED'S Motion For Protective Orderstaying Or, Alternatively, Limiting Discovery Denied. Defendant McCormick & Company, Inc. has not shown that the outcome of the appeal in Environmental Law Foundation v. Beechnut Corporation, (CV-A139821) will affect either the fact discovery or much of the expert discovery to be taken in this litigation such that an order staying or limiting discovery is warranted to serve the interests of justice or promote judicial economy. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: VISHAL GROVER VS. WILLIAM CAMPBELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Defendants Nrt West, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 3: PLAINTIFF VISHAL GROVER'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Defendants NRT West, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Motion to Compel a further discovery response to form interrogatories 15.1 (b) and 15.1 (c) is granted. Defendants shall respond to said interrogatories within fifteen days of the date of service of this Order. Sanctions in the sum of $500.00 are denied because Plaintiff's "meet and confer" letter dated October 29, 2013 made no mention of the Defendant's failure to respond to the specific interrogatories that were deemed inadequate and Defendant served a supplemental interrogatory response based on that communication as to other discovery issues that were raised. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. WINSTON CHOW
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 11: PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Hearing required. No opposition was filed. Plaintiff is required to bring to the hearing a copy of the Answer that was served on it by Defendant, or, if possible, send an electronic copy to contestdept302tr@sftc.org as soon as possible. (See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6C.) In the Court's on-line Register of Actions, the Court has only one page of a two-page Judicial Council form Answer ("Contract"). Defendant checked the box stating that "Defendant admits that all of the statements of the complaint or cross-complaint are true EXCEPT." Nothing else is completed on page 1 of the form Answer, and page 2 was apparently not filed and/or was not scanned on the Court's register of actions. The moving party, however, alludes to something more in the Answer: "Defendant just describes some financial difficulties." (Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, at 2, line 16.) This does not appear on the Answer in the Court's Register of Actions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: XIAOXIAO LI VS. YANTING WANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash/Modify Subpoena Duces Tecum, Request For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 31, 2013, Line 4: DEFENDANT GAOWEI MA'S Motion To Quash/Modify Subpoena Duces Tecum, Request For Attorney's Fees Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Motion to Quash/Modify Subpoena is granted in part and denied in part. The following categories of financial documents which are the subject of a subpoena directed to the Bank of America on the facts set forth meet the balance between Defendant's right of financial privacy and the need for discovery, as follows: monthly account statements, canceled checks, records of deposit and wire transfers, and shall be produced. The time frame for which the bank documents must be produced is limited to the period between 3/14/13 to date. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
